fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 65 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior described in the docstring.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, but the condition `not clamp` causes it to execute only when clamp is False — this constraint flags that the branch condition is inverted relative to the parameter.

spec.expr: not clamp or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True (the else branch of `if not clamp`), the result should be unclamped — but actually it should be clamped; this checks the invariant that is violated by the inverted condition.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After y__5 is finalized, if clamp is True the value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the inverted branch condition causes this to fail.
lo__1 is only computed and used inside the clamping block; if clamp is True this block should not execute, so reaching this point with clamp=True indicates the branch condition is wrong.
